---
title: "Chapter7"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-19"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r}
source('Brownian_motion.R',keep.source = TRUE)
source('EM.R',keep.source = TRUE)
source('plots.R',keep.source = TRUE)
sourceCpp("DoubleWell.cpp") # functions for Strang splitting and likelihood 

########################################################
# Step 1: Definer 0=t_0<t_1<...<t_N=T og drift- og noise-funktioner
########################################################
t_0 <- 0
N <- 600000
h <- 0.0001
T <- N*h
times <- seq(t_0,N*h,length.out=N+1)
# noisy_times <- sort(times + rnorm(length(times), mean = 0, sd = 0.1))

# drift term
F <- function(x,theta = c(0.2, -0.05)){
  eps<-theta[1]; y<-theta[2]
  (x[1]-x[1]^3-y)/eps
}


# additive noise term
G <- function(x, Sigma = c(1.4)) Sigma

########################################################
# Step 3: Euler-Maruyama simulation af SDE løsningen med plots. M_reps repetitions.
########################################################

x0 <- c(0)
n_paths <- 10
paths <- EM_sim_fast(F,G,x0,times,n_paths,progress = TRUE)

# Plot første repetition
#plot_variable_vs_time(paths[[1]],times,to_time = T)

# Definer eventuelt sub paths
ratio <- 100
sub_h <- h*ratio
sub_times <- seq(t_0,N*h,length.out=N/ratio+1)
idx <- seq(1,N+1,by=ratio)
sub_paths <- lapply(paths, function(path) {
  df <- as.data.frame(path)
  # ensure idx are in bounds
  valid_idx <- idx[idx <= nrow(df)]
  df_sub <- df[valid_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  if (ncol(df_sub) == 1) {
    names(df_sub) <- "X"
  }
  df_sub
})
plot_variable_vs_time(sub_paths[[1]],sub_times,T)
#write.csv(bind_rows(sub_paths, .id = "path_id"), "doublewell_paths.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
```{r}
if (sim_new_paths){
  full_paths <- EM_sim_fast(F,G,x0=c(0,0),times,n_paths=15,progress=TRUE)
  paths <- lapply(full_paths, function(path) path[idx,])
  
  #write.csv(bind_rows(sub_paths, .id = "path_id"), "fitzhugh_paths.csv", row.names = FALSE)
}else{
  paths_df <- read.csv("doublewell_paths.csv")
  paths <- paths_df %>%
    split(.$path_id) %>%
    lapply(select, -path_id)
}

path_bias_left <- function(b) bias(b, subset(paths[[1]],X<0)$X, c(0.2, -0.05, 1.4))
path_bias_right <- function(b) bias(b, subset(paths[[1]],X>0)$X, c(0.2, -0.05, 1.4))
path_bias_all <- function(b) bias(b, paths[[1]]$X, c(0.2, -0.05, 1.4))

optimize(path_bias_left,c(-1.2,0))
optimize(path_bias_right,c(0,1.2))
optimize(path_bias_all,c(-1.2,1.2))

b_vals_left <- seq(-1.2, 0, length.out = 200)
y_vals_left <- sapply(b_vals_left, path_bias_left)

b_vals_right <- seq(0, 1.2, length.out = 200)
y_vals_right <- sapply(b_vals_right, path_bias_right)

b_vals_all <- seq(-1.2, 1.2, length.out = 200)
y_vals_all <- sapply(b_vals_all, path_bias_all)



plot(b_vals_left, y_vals_left, type = "l",
     xlab = "b", ylab = "bias",
     main = "Bias as a function of b",
     lwd = 2)
plot(b_vals_right, y_vals_right, type = "l",
     xlab = "b", ylab = "bias",
     main = "Bias as a function of b",
     lwd = 2)
plot(b_vals_all, y_vals_all, type = "l",
     xlab = "b", ylab = "bias",
     main = "Bias as a function of b",
     lwd = 2)
```


```{r}






centers <- c("fix","avg bias")
center_names <- c('Standard fixed point linearization',"global optimal bias")

# True parameter vector (for overlay)
true_params <- c(0.2, -0.05, 1.4)

# Labels for parameters
param_labels <- c("epsilon", "y", "sigma")

# ---------------------- helper: single-center fitting -----------------------
fit_for_center <- function(center_vec, show_progress = FALSE) {
  n_paths <- length(paths)
  preds <- vector("list", n_paths)
  for (i in seq_along(sub_paths)) {
    if (show_progress) message("  fitting path ", i, " / ", n_paths)
    this_path <- sub_paths[[i]]
    par0 <- c(0.1,0,1)
    
    obj <- function(par, path, times) {
      theta_drift <- par[1:2]
      theta_diffusion <- par[3]^2
      b <- optimize(bias,c(-1.2,1.2), as.matrix(path), par)$minimum
      res <- tryCatch(
        log_lik_path_rcpp(as.matrix(path), times, theta_drift,theta_diffusion,
                          method = center_vec,b),
        error = function(e) NULL
      )
      
      if (is.null(res) || !is.finite(res$ll)) {
        return(1e20)
      }
      
      return(res$ll)  
    }
    
    fit <- optim(par    = par0,
                 fn     = obj,
                 path   = this_path,
                 times  = sub_times,
                 method = "Nelder-Mead",
                 control = list(maxit = 5000, trace = 0)
    )
    
    # recompute sigma at optimum
    # final_res <- log_lik_path_rcpp(as.matrix(this_path),
    #                               sub_times,
    #                               fit$par[1:2],
    #                               method = center_vec,
    #                               sigma = sigma_mle)
    
    # sigma_mle <- final_res$sigma # updated via MLE
    preds[[i]] <- fit$par
  }
  
  # Convert results to data.frame
  preds_mat <- do.call(rbind, preds)
  colnames(preds_mat) <- param_labels
  out_df <- as.data.frame(preds_mat)
  return(out_df)
}

# ---------------------- run fits for all centers -----------------------
all_results_list <- vector("list", length(centers))

for (i in seq_along(centers)) {
  cname <- center_names[i]
  cent  <- centers[[i]]
  message("Running fits for center: ", cname)
  
  res_df <- fit_for_center(cent, show_progress = TRUE)
  
  # add run index and center label
  res_df <- res_df %>% 
    mutate(run = seq_len(nrow(res_df)), 
           center = cname)
  
  all_results_list[[i]] <- res_df
}

# Combine results and remove rows where all parameters are NA
all_results <- bind_rows(all_results_list)
all_results <- all_results %>% 
  filter(!apply(select(., all_of(param_labels)), 1, function(r) all(is.na(r))))

est_df <- all_results %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(param_labels),
               names_to = "param",
               values_to = "estimate") %>%
  mutate(param = factor(param, levels = param_labels),
         center = factor(center, levels = center_names))

# ---------------------- use your new plotting function -----------------------
p <- plot_estimates(
  est_df       = est_df,
  true_params  = true_params,
  param_labels = param_labels,
  center_names = center_names,
  title_text   = "Parameter Estimates Across Methods",
  subtitle     = "SDE estimation comparison"
)

print(p)
```
